steps.title: Find the antecedent of the blank
steps.content: The sentence introduces the judges for the 1974 Nobel Prize in Literature as the subject making a decision. The blank must be a pronoun that points to the later noun phrase “the judges” — a plural noun. The pronoun in the blank must be plural to agree with that noun phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each pronoun against the plural antecedent “the judges”:
  • Choice A — you: Second-person pronoun; does not refer to “the judges” at all. ✗
  • Choice B — it: Singular pronoun; disagrees with the plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — anyone: Singular indefinite pronoun; disagrees with the plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — they: Plural pronoun; agrees with the plural “the judges.” ✓
That confirms D is correct.
